rationale: Cohesive end-to-end removal of three dead/inert `app_settings` columns. New alembic revision `d1b6e2g04c38_drop_dead_app_settings_columns.py` uses `op.batch_alter_table('app_settings')` to drop `invoice_commission_percent`, `min_deposit`, `min_withdraw`, with a symmetric `downgrade` that restores the columns at the H-2 widened precision and original `server_default`s. `backend/app/models.py` deletes the three `mapped_column` declarations on `AppSettings`; `backend/app/schemas.py` removes them from `AdminSettingsOut`/`AdminSettingsUpdateIn` and the now-empty `_min_ok` validator; `backend/app/routers/admin/settings.py` drops them from `_EDITABLE_FIELDS` and `_to_out`; `backend/app/seed.py` no longer seeds the inert defaults; `backend/app/money.py` docstring is updated with the historical note. Frontend `AdminSettingsPage.tsx` removes the three corresponding `<Row>/<NumberField>` widgets, and `AdminSettingsDto`/`AdminSettingsUpdateBody` in `frontend/src/api/types.ts` plus the regenerated `openapi.json`/`openapi.generated.ts` reflect the removal. The deleted `test_app_settings_min_deposit_and_min_withdraw_round_trip_above_1e10` test (and the `AppSettings` import line) is the only test removed, with a replacement NOTE comment pointing to the still-passing `test_currency_min_deposit_and_min_withdraw_round_trip_above_1e10` for the genuine per-currency enforcement points. The test-relaxation/assertion-strip detector firings are false positives: the deleted test guarded round-trip precision of a column that no longer exists, so removing it is the correct schema-aligned action, not a cheat to mask failure.
